topics.title: Basic Sentence Structure
topics.explanation:
Georgian follows Subject-Object-Verb (SOV) order, which is different from English.

Example: 'I bread eat' instead of 'I eat bread'

Georgian does not use articles (the/a). Context determines definiteness.

examples.english: I study Georgian. (lit: I Georgian study)
He/she reads a book. (lit: He/she book reads)

quiz.question: What is the basic word order in Georgian?
Does Georgian have articles like 'the' and 'a'?
